12	    "description": "\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nReview area: execute-bead merge eligibility semantics. 30:type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
31-	BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
32-	AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`

35-	ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
36-
37-	// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
38-	// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
39-	// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
40-	// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
41-	Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
42-	Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
43-	Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
44-
45-	// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
46-	Reason              string            `json:"reason,omitempty"`
47-	PreserveRef         string            `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
48-	GateResults         []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
49-	RequiredExecSummary string            `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
50-	ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
51-	// Ratchet fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult when declarative
52-	// ratchet thresholds were evaluated during landing. HELIX and other
53-	// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from
54-	// generic execution failures.
55-	RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
56-	RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
57-
58-	// NoChangesRationale is populated when outcome == task_no_changes and the
59-	// agent wrote a rationale file to the execution bundle dir inside the
60-	// worktree. It carries the agent's explanation of why no commits were made.
61-	NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
62-
63-	// NoEvidencePaths names worktree paths that remained dirty when the agent

65-	// operators diagnose silent commit failures before the worktree is cleaned up.
66-	NoEvidencePaths []string `json:"no_evidence_paths,omitempty"`

95	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead.
96	// Implemented by *bead.Store — kept as a minimal interface so the agent
97	// package does not need to import a concrete store type in tests.
98	type BeadEventAppender interface {
99		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
100	}
101	
102	// ExecuteBeadRuntime carries the non-durable plumbing for an execute-bead
103	// run: per-invocation intent (FromRev, PromptFile, WorkerID) and
104	// non-serializable injection seams (BeadEvents, Service, AgentRunner).
105	// Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
106	// ContextBudget, MirrorCfg) live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed
107	// via ExecuteBeadWithConfig's rcfg argument.
108	//
109	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 3.
110	type ExecuteBeadRuntime struct {
111		FromRev     string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
112		PromptFile  string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
113		WorkerID    string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
114		BeadEvents  BeadEventAppender
115		Service     agentlib.FizeauService
116		AgentRunner AgentRunner
117	}
118	
119	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
120	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
121	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
122	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
123	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
124	// worktree.
125	type GitOps interface {
126		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
127		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
128		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
129		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
130		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
131		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
132		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
133		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
134		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
135		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
136		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
137		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
138		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
139		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
140		// other work that might prune it.
141		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
142		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
143		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
144		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
145	}
146

149		Run(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error)

244	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
245	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
246	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
247	const (
248		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded  = "task_succeeded"
249		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed     = "task_failed"
250		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges  = "task_no_changes"
251		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence = "task_no_evidence"
252	)
253	
254	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
255	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
256	const (
257		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
258		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
259		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
260		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
261		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
262		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
263		ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict               = "push_conflict"
264		ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview       = "preserved_needs_review"
265		ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced         = "no_evidence_produced"
266		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
267		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
268		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
269	
270		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
271		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
272		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
273		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
274		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
275		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
276		//
277		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
278		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
279		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
280		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
281		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
282		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
283	
284		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
285		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
286		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
287		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
288		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
289	
290		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop
291		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
292		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
293		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
294		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
295	
296		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman is set when the focused
297		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
298		// human judgment, not automated retry. Parked under the same short
299		// cooldown with a kind:land-conflict-needs-human event.
300		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman = "land_conflict_needs_human"
301	)
302	
303	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
304	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
305	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
306	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
307		if exitCode != 0 {
308			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
309		}
310	
311		switch outcome {
312		case "merged":
313			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
314			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
315			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
316			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
317			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
318			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
319				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
320			}
321			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
322				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
323			}
324			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
325		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
326			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
327		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
328			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
329		case "error":
330			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
331		case "preserved":
332			switch reason {
333			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
334				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
335			case "post-run checks failed":
336				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
337			case RatchetPreserveReason:
338				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
339			default:
340				if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
341					return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
342				}
343				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
344				// explicitly requested no merge.
345				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
346			}
347		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
348			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
349		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
350			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
351		default:
352			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
353		}
354	}
355	
356	func isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason string) bool {
357		for _, prefix := range []string{
358			"large-deletion gate:",
359			"syntax sanity gate:",

9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.

12	type Bead struct {

15		Status    string    `json:"status"`

22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)

25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.

35		// are stored here and written back on save.

37	}

1	// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
17	// matches the scanner in the agent package.

data.text:
Committed. Created `cli/internal/agentmetrics/` with:
- `types.go` — `Attempt` struct, `Source*` constants
- `bucket.go` — `Bucket` taxonomy + `ClassifyBucket()` aligned with `agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*`; `already_satisfied` maps to `BucketSuccess` per locked decision
- `loader.go` — `LoadAttempts()` reads FEAT-010 `.ddx/exec/runs/*.json` (try-layer) first, falls back to `.ddx/executions/*/result.json`, dedupes by AttemptID (Story 8.0 / `state_runs.go` semantics — run-store wins on collision), enriches missing harness/provider/model from `kind:routing` and `kind:escalation-summary` bead events
- `bucket_test.go` — pins every status → bucket mapping incl. `already_satisfied → BucketSuccess`
- `loader_test.go` — covers bundle-only path, multi-source dedupe (run-store wins, run-layer noise ignored, bundle-only included), routing enrichment of legacy bundles, no-overwrite of explicit harness, empty-dirs no-error
